text.title: Rust Ownership Model Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules for rust-ownership. Each rule pairs binary check + sourced rationale. Last rule is the terminal skip-this-methodology guard.

rule.statement: Each value has exactly one owner; transfer ownership via move, not via clone.
rule.rationale: Cloning copies data and obscures who is responsible for drop.

rule.statement: Borrows are either multiple shared (&T) OR one exclusive (&mut T) at any time; combine carefully across scopes.
rule.rationale: Mixing shared + exclusive at the same time produces aliasing UB and the borrow checker rejects it.

rule.statement: Calling .clone() to silence the borrow checker is forbidden; instead lift the value into a longer-lived scope or take a borrow.
rule.rationale: clone() papers over design issues, allocating extra memory and obscuring ownership.

rule.statement: Rc<T> and Rc<RefCell<T>> MUST NOT cross thread boundaries (spawn, tokio::spawn); use Arc<Mutex<T>> or Arc<RwLock<T>>.
rule.rationale: Rc/RefCell are !Send + !Sync; using them across threads breaks soundness.

rule.statement: When a fn returns a reference, lifetime parameters are explicit; never rely on elision for non-trivial signatures.
rule.rationale: Implicit elision occasionally infers the wrong lifetime and produces hard-to-debug compile errors.

rule.statement: If the preconditions in `Applies If` fail or any of the `Skip If` conditions is true, return `skipped` and apply no further rules.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are not universal; running them outside their fit-window wastes effort and produces wrong outputs.
